content_of_post_to_comment:
Hi! English isn't my native language, but almost everything in my daily work is in English. Presentations, readings, meetings. I'm currently at a B2 level and have a good vocabulary in my field of work. I didn't have the chance to study English formally because it is expensive on my country. My dream is to feel confident enough to do an exchange program. So, I've decided to start doing as many things as possible in English and find opportunities to talk with people about non-work. My first step is reading a book in English (I enjoy reading). I tried three times before, but I always gave up. This time, though, I'm committed to finishing it.

P.S. English is a very sensitive topic for me because, even though I've tried every free or low-cost way to study it, I see every day at work how much faster people who have more money, and therefore access to better opportunities, can improve.
